probes.name: platform-user-catalogue-seeded
probes.description: The platform_users table has at least one row in status='active' with department='ops' AND at least one row in status='active' with `super_admin` role assignment. Ensures the ops-support + super_admin baseline is provisioned before the app opens for platform-admin traffic. Fail = the platform_admin surface is unusable (no one can log in as a real staffer).

probes.name: platform-user-mfa-coverage
probes.description: Every ACTIVE PlatformUser has an Identity with mfa_secret_encrypted NOT NULL. Non-critical because the middleware refuses non-MFA tokens at request time — but a red probe flags an operational drift (an Identity's MFA was disabled without the corresponding PlatformUser being suspended).

probes.name: platform-user-manager-integrity
probes.description: Zero PlatformUser rows have manager_id pointing at a soft-deleted or offboarded PlatformUser. If nonzero, the org-chart accessor will resolve to phantom rows during redaction. Fail = a super_admin should run `platform-user:reassign-manager` on the affected rows.

probes.name: platform-user-org-chart-cycles
probes.description: Zero cycles in the org chart (following manager_id from any row eventually terminates at NULL or the same row). The reassignment observer refuses cycles at write time — this probe defends against pre-existing bad data (imported org charts, migration bugs).

probes.name: platform-user-on-call-resolver-reachable
probes.description: OnCallResolver responds within `platform-user.on_call.pagerduty.timeout_seconds`. When the backend is `internal`, checks the on_call_rotation_entries table is queryable. When the backend is `pagerduty`, GETs /oncalls with the schedule id filter. Non-critical because escalation has a fallback path (Slack broadcast to the department channel), but a red probe warns that PagerDuty pages may not reach the right person.

probes.name: platform-user-offboarding-sla-track
probes.description: Zero PlatformUser rows have status='offboarded' AND offboarded_at older than `platform-user.offboarding.sla_hours` (default 24h) AND no successful RevokePlatformUserAccessJob metric emitted. CRITICAL because SOC 2 CC6.7 depends on prompt access revocation — a red probe means the SLA is currently breached and PagerDuty pages fire.

probes.name: platform-user-retention-hold-integrity
probes.description: Zero PlatformUser rows have deleted_at older than `platform-user.retention.hold_days + 7 days` (a 7-day grace beyond the configured hold). If nonzero, PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob is stalled and rows are lingering past the retention window. A red probe warns compliance + ops.

probes.name: platform-user-identity-uniqueness
probes.description: Zero Identity rows reference MORE than one platform_users row. Defends against a bug where the UNIQUE(identity_id) constraint was dropped in a bad migration. Fail = catastrophic invariant break (two PlatformUsers sharing credentials).
